label: Trapezius strain without rupture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated trapezius strain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed trapezius strain. We did not find fracture, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection, or another emergency pathway today.
home: Rest the sore area as instructed.
Use ice, heat, gentle motion, posture changes, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Avoid activity that causes sharp or worsening pain until you are improving.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not combine medicines unless your clinician said it is safe.
return: Worsening severe pain, new weakness, numbness, color change, or trouble using the arm.
New trauma, deformity, chest pain, trouble breathing, or neurologic symptoms.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated trapezius strain.
No fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection, cardiopulmonary red flags, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, severe pain, neurovascular compromise, infection concern, cardiopulmonary red flags, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports sprain/strain home-care framing and warning signs. Autonomous expansion approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed trapezius strain without fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection, cardiopulmonary red flags, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated trapezius strain
no fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection, cardiopulmonary red flags, or specialist-directed plan documented
